case_title: NINA ISMAILOFF VS. DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES
cause_of_action: WRITS OF MANDATE OR PROH., CERTI., ETC./ADMIN. AGEN

docket_entries.description: NOTICE OF HEARING ON PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE, PROOF OF SERVICE FILED BY PETITIONER ISMAILOFF, NINA HEARING SET FOR FEB-27-2007 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT RESPONDENT DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES RECOVER FROM PETITIONER ISMAILOFF, NINA JUDGMENT DENYING PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE. SUSPENSION OF PETITIONER'S LICENSE IS TO REMAIN IN EFFECT. COSTS FOR FILING FEE AWARDED TO DMV AND TO PAID BY PETITIONER NINA ISMAILOFF FOR REMITTANCE BY RESPONDENT OF SF COUNTY AS PROVIDED BY GC 6103.5. SEE SCANNED DOC
NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER/NOTICE OF RULING FILED FILED BY RESPONDENT DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES
ANSWER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION FILED BY RESPONDENT DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES

LAW AND MOTION, 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PETITIONER NINA ISMAILOFF'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE IS DENIED. THE PETITION IS UNTIMELY UNDER C.C.P. SECTION 1094.5. EVEN IF IT WERE TIMELY, THE DECISION OF THE D.M.V. WAS REASONABLE WHETHER REVIEWED UNDER C.C.P. SECTION 1094.5 OR C.C.P. SECTION 1085. COUNSEL FOR MOVING PARTY DID NOT GIVE NOTICE TO OPPOSING COUNSEL OF HIS INTENT TO APPEAR THERFORE THE COURT COULD NOT HEAR ARGUMENT ON THE MOTION. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR# 8600
MINI-MINUTES FOR MAR-05-2007 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
ORDER DENYING PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE
MINI-MINUTES FOR FEB-27-2007 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER NINA ISMAILOFF'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: CONTINUED TO MARCH 5, 2007. MOVING PARTY TO PROVIDE COURTESY COPIES AND COVER LETTER REFLECTING NEW HEARING DATE. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

Off Calendar Mar-05-2007 Denied
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCE:
E. Michael Linscheid, Esq., 415-782-6002 for Petitioner and moving party Nina Ismailoff

RULING:
NO OTHER APPEARANCES. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING.
PETITIONER NINA ISMAILOFF'S Petition For Writ Of Mandate IS DENIED. THE PETITION IS UNTIMELY UNDER C.C.P. SECTION 1094.5. EVEN IF IT WERE TIMELY, THE DECISION OF THE D.M.V. WAS REASONABLE WHETHER REVIEWED UNDER C.C.P. SECTION 1094.5 OR C.C.P. SECTION 1085. COUNSEL FOR MOVING PARTY DID NOT GIVE NOTICE TO OPPOSING COUNSEL OF HIS INTENT TO APPEAR THERFORE THE COURT COULD NOT HEAR ARGUMENT ON THE MOTION. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR# 8600 (302/PJM/JM)
